verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 (C19orf36) encodes a member of the IZUMO family, but unlike IZUMO1 (the well-characterized sperm-surface fusogen) it lacks a transmembrane domain and GPI signal. UniProt Q1ZYL8 annotates IZUMO4 as a nuclear-localized protein, consistent with the NCBI summary ('Located in nucleus'). No surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact cells, or surface proteomics data supports PM display. Checking contextual buckets: cell-state-induced surface display — no evidence; tissue-restricted surface — no data showing any surface pool; lysosomal exocytosis — no lysosomal targeting signals reported; dual localization — no PM pool documented; stable surface attachment — no TM anchor or GPI signal to mediate anchoring. No therapeutic program targets IZUMO4 on the cell surface. The protein therefore appears strictly nuclear with no credible route to the extracellular face.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Experimental surface proteomics or flow data for IZUMO4 are absent; nuclear annotation is bioinformatic/database-level. If a nuclear localization signal is absent and the protein is small, cytoplasmic/nuclear ambiguity remains.
